strengths: Direct AI agent and MCP infrastructure experience at current role — rare alignment with the job's AI agent development requirement
Deep TypeScript and Node.js expertise across multiple production roles, closely matching the primary stack
15+ years of fullstack breadth across web, backend, mobile, and embedded — well exceeds the 6-year minimum with demonstrated seniority
concerns: No mention of NestJS anywhere in the resume despite it being the primary backend framework for this role
Kotlin/Java exposure is superficial — only briefly mentioned for robotic arms tooling, not as a backend service language
Testing experience centers on custom D-lang tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) rather than the required Jest, Vitest, or Playwright
response.justification: Bogdan is a strong fullstack generalist with directly relevant AI agent infrastructure work and solid TypeScript/Node.js production history, both of which align well with the role's primary stack and emerging requirements. However, NestJS — the core backend framework — is entirely absent from his resume, and his Kotlin/Java exposure is too shallow to cover the secondary stack comfortably. His testing background is notable but built around non-standard tooling rather than the specified frameworks. Overall a promising candidate whose fit hinges on how quickly he can close the NestJS gap and whether the AI agent work at re:cinq is substantive enough to offset the secondary stack weaknesses.
